title: BUG: diagonal facings wrong (NW shows SE; SE shows SW) for dinos AND backhoe
description: User screenshots: purple stego faces SE while moving NW (180 flip); player+dinos face SW when moving SE (adjacent swap). Cardinals are correct (tests cover them) — DIAGONAL mapping is broken somewhere: Direction8 sector->Dir8 for diagonals, importer's mirrored-sprite assignment (SW/W/NW from SE/E/NE), or array index order. Investigate against actual sprite files; fix; EXTEND FacingCorrectness to all 8 directions (diagonal legs) so this can never regress.

comments.author: Greg Back
comments.text:
ROOT CAUSE (art data, not code). Each non-front facing is produced INDEPENDENTLY by
img2img-rotating the front (S) reference with an AMBIGUOUS, character-relative prompt in
Tools/generate_sprites.py ("...we see its front AND its RIGHT side"). The image model
read "its right side" inconsistently -- often as the character's ANATOMICAL right, which
lands on SCREEN-LEFT -- so a subset of generated facings came out horizontally MIRRORED
vs the compass name in their filename. Because the slicer builds the left set (SW/W/NW)
by flipping the right set (SE/E/NE), a mirrored source silently swaps the whole pair.

This is NOT a math bug. Verified: Direction8.FromVector maps diagonals correctly
((1,-1)->SE, (-1,1)->NW, (1,1)->NE, (-1,-1)->SW), the slicer's SE->SW/E->W/NE->NW mirror
is geometrically correct, and SW/W/NW PNGs are EXACT pixel-mirrors of SE/E/NE (meanAbsDiff
0.00 for all actors). The FacingCorrectness test passed on diagonals-would-have-passed
because it only checks Dir8-index<->array-slot consistency, which was always correct -- it
has no way to SEE which way the art points. That is why the defect was invisible to tests.

The mislabeling is PER-(actor,facing), NOT a uniform swap. Evidence from the unambiguous
sprites (backhoe loader/cab-face; dino snouts/beaks/frills), eyeballed at high resolution:
  backhoe_SE  -> faces SW (face+loader point down-LEFT)      [SWAP]
  backhoe_NE  -> faces NW (loader up-left, taillight to cam) [SWAP]
  triceratops_SE -> faces SW (beak points LEFT)              [SWAP]
  triceratops_NE -> faces NE (beak points RIGHT)             [CORRECT]  <- same actor, opposite!
  trex_SE / spinosaurus_SE / brachiosaurus_SE / velociraptor_SE / parasaurolophus_SE
              -> faces SE (snout RIGHT)                       [CORRECT]
  stegosaurus_SE -> faces SW (snout LEFT)                    [SWAP]
  ankylosaurus_E/SE/NE -> all face LEFT                       [SWAP] (whole actor mirrored;
              even the E cardinal profile is flipped)

FIX (two layers, no regeneration required):
1) GeneratedArtImporter.cs -- added a per-actor facing-correction table (FlippedFacingPairs)
   + AdultSuffix(). Because W/SW/NW are exact mirrors of E/SE/NE, the correctly-oriented
   sprite for a mirrored facing ALREADY EXISTS as its partner file, so a flipped slot is
   corrected by loading the partner (a per-actor compass horizontal pair-swap). Only
   HIGH-CONFIDENCE flips are listed so the fix can NEVER regress an already-correct actor:
     backhoe {SE,NE}, triceratops {SE}, stegosaurus {SE}, ankylosaurus {E,SE,NE}.
   Applied to adult idle + adult strides + backhoe rolls (they inherit each facing's
   handedness via the same suffix path -- covers every array the importer fills for those
   actors). Verified visually: post-fix, all 8 backhoe slots face their compass label and
   the stego SE/SW pair is corrected.
2) Tools/generate_sprites.py -- rewrote ROTATE to be SCREEN-relative and unambiguous
   (head/front points to the frame's right, rotation pinned toward the frame RIGHT) plus a
   NO_MIRROR clause, and documented that the slicer MIRROR step is correct but depends on
   source orientation. This is the permanent root-cause fix; regenerating with it yields a
   consistent set, after which the importer table should be emptied.

TESTS: Case_FacingCorrectness extended from 4 cardinals to all 8 headings. Added
FindClearStraightTarget (arbitrary heading + CORRIDOR line-of-sight, so diagonal legs
drive straight instead of staircasing) and RelocateForEightWay/RankEightWay (seek open
ground covering both axes AND both diagonal hands). Coverage now requires both axes + >=2
diagonals. Added DinoDiagonalSpotCheck: a follower dino must index a diagonal facing to the
right slot (idle or either stride phase). NOTE documented in-test: this guards the Dir8
sector math + array indexing only -- it cannot detect art-orientation flips.

RESIDUAL / FOLLOW-UP (not a regression -- these keep current behavior, just not yet
corrected): baby/kid stage sets are SEPARATE generations and were NOT audited; a few adult
NE rear-views (brachiosaurus, pteranodon, parasaurolophus) and pteranodon_SE were too
ambiguous to classify at high confidence and were left untouched. The clean permanent fix
for all of these is to regenerate with the corrected prompt (Tools/generate_sprites.py) and
then empty the importer's FlippedFacingPairs table.

title: BUG: duplicate species eggs (2 T-Rex) — uniqueness race
description: User dug 2 T-Rex eggs. Root cause hypothesis: TryRollUnownedEggSpecies checks OWNED (hatched) dinos only — multiple eggs rolled before the first HATCHES can all pick the same species. Fix: reserve species when the egg DugItemInfo is created (pending set: buried eggs + spilled unhatched eggs), release on hatch/destroy; roll excludes owned+pending. Test: dig two egg batches without hatching -> species differ; also UniqueDinoNoDupes extension.

comments.author: Greg Back
comments.text:
FIXED — duplicate-egg-species race (2 T-Rex).

ROOT CAUSE (confirmed): TryRollUnownedEggSpecies excluded only OWNED = HATCHED
species (OwnedEggSpecies iterates _dinos, the live DinoControllers). An egg that
was dug but not yet hatched is a live ItemPickup, never in _dinos. FinishDig
resolves the WHOLE batch (GameManager.cs loop calling ResolveDugItem) BEFORE any
pickup spawns/hatches, so two eggs in one batch both saw the same "owned" set and
Random.Range could pick the same unowned species -> 2 T-Rex. Same hole across two
quick digs and for spilled-but-un-hatched eggs.

FIX — pending-species reservation set in GameManager:
- Dictionary<DinoType,int> _reservedEggSpecies (ref-counted, defence in depth).
- TryRollUnownedEggSpecies now excludes OWNED *and* RESERVED species.
- ResolveDugItem RESERVES the species the instant an egg is finalized (after the
  unique re-roll), so a second egg in the same batch / a later dig cannot pick it.
- No-species-remaining fallback: if EggSpeciesAllOwned() (nerf active) -> shard
  (else treasure), else -> FRUIT. Rationale: shards must never drop before every
  egg species is owned, so a batch that merely exhausts the *reserved* pool spills
  fruit rather than leaking an early shard.

RELEASE POINTS:
- HatchEgg: releases the reservation and takes ownership (SpawnDino) in one
  synchronous step — no gap for a duplicate. Direct HatchEgg (no pickup) = no-op.
- ItemPickup.OnDestroy: releases for an egg destroyed WITHOUT hatching (dig
  re-entry, reset); guarded with _hatched flag + !Application.isPlaying so
  play-mode-exit / domain-reload teardown is a safe no-op.
- TestReset: _reservedEggSpecies.Clear().

AUDIT of every egg-DugItemInfo path: FinishDig->ResolveDugItem (reserves) OK;
SpawnRewardPickup now routes egg type through ResolveDugItem (duck reward is only
fruit/treasure today, but the gate is closed); DropTreeFruit makes Fruit only (no
eggs) OK; TestSpawnItem reserves egg species for the pickup lifetime. PEEK during
digging is cosmetic (site-roll tint) and unchanged — two buried eggs at ONE site
now DO get distinct species because FinishDig re-rolls each through the reserving
ResolveDugItem.

TESTS (IntegrationTestCases.Case_UniqueDinoNoDupes extended): resolve a 6-egg
batch with nothing owned -> assert 4 DISTINCT egg species + 2 fruit fallbacks + 0
shard/treasure leak; assert 4 reservations held; reset; assert reservations
cleared; then the original partial/full owned-exclusion assertions. New hooks:
TestResolveDugBatch (mirrors FinishDig, keeps reservations), TestReservedEggSpeciesCount.
TestRollDugItem now releases the egg it samples so the sampling distribution stays
stationary (keeps existing UniqueDinoNoDupes/ShardDropRate/ShardHatchCeremony green).
FeedAndGrow/EggHatch spawn via TestSpawnItem: fruit un-affected; the egg reserves
then releases on hatch.

FILES: Assets/Scripts/Core/GameManager.cs, Assets/Scripts/Overworld/ItemPickup.cs,
Assets/Scripts/Testing/IntegrationTestCases.cs. (Not compiled/run here — no Unity
MCP; rerun DinoDigger/Run Integration Tests to verify.)

title: Fruit Stand: first functional building, converts fruit to coins
description: Buildings are inert scenery and fruit has no sink. Make building index 1 a Fruit Stand: once finished, tapping loose fruit when no dino is hungry sends a resident/courier to carry it to the stand where it banks as +1 coin, with every 5th sale popping a gem worth 3. Reuses Trike courier walk, ItemPickup carry, CollectTreasure. Gives buildings purpose and roughly triples effective coin income per dig site.
close_reason: Fruit Stand live: resident haul or self-sell fallback, 5th-sale gem, glut guard widened; double-green 47/47 x2

title: Town: wire remaining plots and persist town state
description: Phase 1 only places the first plot (Pebble Playground) and town progress is not saved. Wire the remaining plots in TownArea/SceneBuilder so the 10-600 price queue can actually run, and persist next-building index plus per-building construction state in SaveData (schema bump v4). Prerequisite for functional buildings (Fruit Stand, Recess Time).
close_reason: 9 plots wired, town persists in save v4, double-green 46/46 x2

title: Economy: downgrade fruit dig rolls to treasure when nothing is hungry
description: Fruit is 40 percent of dig drops but demand is finite (5 fruit per dino, Big dinos stop eating), so fruit piles up useless. Mirror the existing egg/shard downgrade in GameManager.ResolveDugItem: when no dino IsHungry, convert a configurable fraction (default 0.75, config knob FruitDowngradeFraction) of fruit rolls to treasure. Keep the trickle so tree-shake and feeding still exist. Later issues (snack builders, fruit stand) will widen the demand condition.

title: Economy: treasure denominations (gem=3, bone=2)
description: Every treasure banks +1 regardless of sprite variant, making coins too slow for the 10-600 building price curve. Add GameConfig.TreasureValues per variant (coin=1, gem=3, boot=1, bone=2), carry the value through DugItemInfo/ItemPickup, and bank it in GameManager.CollectTreasure with counter feedback reflecting the amount. Files: Assets/Scripts/Config/GameConfig.cs, Assets/Scripts/Dig/DigModeController.cs (RollItem), Assets/Scripts/Overworld/ItemPickup.cs, Assets/Scripts/Core/GameManager.cs.

title: Builder dinos need construction-worker look: hard hat + cute tool while building
description: Greg playtested Phase 1: building looks good but builder dinos show no sign of being construction workers. Pull the Phase-2 hard-hat/tool slice forward: yellow hard-hat overlay anchored to the builder's head during commute+work, small stone tool at the site, removed on clock-out. Overlay approach (not per-species art regen) for cost; toy-box style is forgiving.
close_reason: Hard-hat + stone-mallet overlays on builders (state-derived visibility, all exit paths covered), striped barrier sign at active sites with pop-out on finish. Art: 3 props gen'd (~$0.18). Suite 45/45 double-green with new prop assertions. Visually verified in-editor demo.

title: Town not wired in built scene — tests masked it via TestInstallTown
description: SceneBuilder CreateTown added TownArea by reflection, never Configured it, and WireOptional silently dropped the TownArea->TownController type mismatch into GameManager._town. Scene shipped with 0 TownController; real players' coins would never build. Found during post-Phase-1 reflective-wiring cleanup. Fix: direct AddComponent<TownArea>+Configure, create+Configure TownController, strict Wire; importer direct lib.BuildingStates; new TownWiredInScene test case asserting the scene ships a live configured town.
close_reason: Fixed: SceneBuilder now AddComponent<TownArea>+Configure(4 plots)+AddComponent<TownController>+Configure, strict Wire into GameManager._town; importer assigns lib.BuildingStates directly; all reflection removed. Also fixed stranded-builder bug (StopWork no-oped for commuting builders — DinoController _headingToWork flag). New TownWiredInScene case proves the scene ships a live town. Double-green gate 45/45 x2.

title: P1b: Building states + builder NPC loop + celebration
description: BuildingController: 4 construction state visuals -> finished (placeholder art from PlaceholderArtGenerator for tests; real art via P1c). Resident (non-buddy) dinos commute from the meadow to the site, work (existing dance/bob anims for now), advance states over time+coins, celebrate on completion (confetti+sting), then resume meadow life. Buddies and the PLAYER are never drafted. Tests: BuildAdvancesThroughStates, BuilderCommutesFromMeadow, BuildingFinishesAndCelebrates.

comments.author: Greg Back
comments.text: Implemented (write-only, no Unity MCP). BuildingController (Assets/Scripts/Overworld/BuildingController.cs) is a pure view+progress state machine: construction states 0..3 then finished, sprite from PlaceholderLibrary.BuildingStates[5] (null-tolerant). TownController drafts up to GameConfig.TownMaxBuilders (2) NON-BUDDY meadow residents from GameManager.TownAcquireBuilders, sends each to commute via the existing DinoController.WalkTo/BFS, then a new DinoController Work mode (reuses busy patterns; stays put + bobs, never wanders home). Worked time (dt * on-site builder count) advances one state per GameConfig.TownSecondsPerBuildState (~8s); each boundary raises GameEvents.BuildingStateAdvanced. On completion: confetti (reused SpawnConfetti) + Grow sting + GameEvents.BuildingFinished, then builders Dance and trot home. If no residents exist construction simply WAITS. Placeholder art: PlaceholderArtGenerator.WriteBuildingState draws the 5-state stone/log/leaf silhouette arc. No save persistence (deferred); TestReset wipes sites cleanly. Tests: BuildAdvancesThroughStates, BuilderCommutesFromMeadow (asserts crew are non-buddy residents that leave the meadow), BuildingFinishesAndCelebrates.

title: P1a: Town economy — wallet, build queue, auto-start
description: Coins auto-spend: when TreasureCount >= next building price, construction auto-starts (no player input). Build-queue service in a new TownController; fixed curated order (Pebble Playground first, ~10 coins). Coins deduct as spent. NEVER touches BackhoeController or player input. Test: CoinsAutoSpendStartsBuild, PlayerControlUnaffectedByBuild (tap-to-move works normally mid-construction; backhoe never auto-moves to the site).

comments.author: Greg Back
comments.text: Implemented (write-only, no Unity MCP). Town economy + build-queue landed in a new TownController (Assets/Scripts/Overworld/TownController.cs). It polls the treasure wallet each GameManager tick (and on TreasureCollected) and, with ZERO player input, auto-starts the next building the moment the wallet clears its curated price: deducts the coins via GameManager.TownTrySpend (save written + counter refreshed), breaks ground on the next free TownArea plot, and raises GameEvents.TownBuildStarted. Curated price curve in GameConfig.TownBuildingPrices = {10,25,50,90,150,240,380,490,600} (from the design doc roster; phase 1 uses [0]=10). Money flows through a single GameManager hook so there is one source of truth. BackhoeController / player input untouched. Tests: CoinsAutoSpendStartsBuild (grant coins -> site appears, price deducted to 0, event fired), PlayerControlUnaffectedByBuild (backhoe holds position for 2s of active construction, tap-to-move still works, dig entry still works, buddy keeps following). Both in Assets/Scripts/Testing/IntegrationTestCasesTown.cs.

title: Dino Town: HTML design document
description: Rich self-contained HTML pitch/design doc using real game sprites: concept, toddler design principles (no input needed, ambient delight), economy flow (coins auto-spent), builder-dino behavior loop (commute from meadow, hard hats, tools, construction animations), building roster with interactions, construction-state progression (3+ states), town layout on the island, art pipeline plan (img2img from existing style), phased implementation plan with ticket breakdown. Published as artifact.

comments.author: Greg Back
comments.text:
Dino Town design document complete. Rich self-contained HTML pitch page (0.70 MB) at scratchpad/dino-town-design.html — awaiting orchestrator review before publishing as an artifact.

Covers all 9 required sections: (1) one-line hero pitch, (2) toddler zero-direction rationale + dead-end-counter conversion, (3) economy flow with price curve 1-8 (first building ~10 coins per the shard-pacing lesson) and optional tap-to-cheer, (4) builder-dino loop (commute/hard-hats/tools/work/celebrate, big-dino-builds-faster), (5) 9-building roster (Greg's library/coffee/cinema + Pebble Playground, Bone-anza Bowling, Dino Daycare, Tar-Pit Springs, Gronk's Grocer, Fossil Fountain), (6) 4-state construction progression, (7) 48x48 island town district + mound/stream exclusion zones (canvas map), (8) img2img art pipeline plan (~110 images, ~$3.96 at $0.036/img), (9) 3-phase beads breakdown with integration-suite test-case names.

Uses 14 real game sprites (dinos/backhoe/treasure/eggs/duck) downscaled to 180px and embedded as base64 (0.49 MB). Building concepts are CSS/SVG-lite (no new AI art). Toy-box art direction matched (chunky, saturated, thick dark outlines, exact game palette). Light+dark themed via token system, responsive (no h-overflow at 390px), reduced-motion respected. Verified in-browser both themes + mobile.

title: Shard egg pacing: first egg 5 shards, then 8/15/20
description: First shard-built egg takes too long at flat 20. New progression per hatched shard egg: 5, 8, 15, 20 (then 20 for the 5th if ever). Applies to nest assembly thresholds (5 visual states scale to current requirement), ceremony trigger, save v3 compat (NestSpeciesQueue/shard count already banked — count SHARD EGGS HATCHED to pick the requirement). Update NestAssembly/ShardHatchCeremony tests.

comments.author: Greg Back
comments.text:
Implemented escalating shard-egg progression.

DESIGN
- Requirement now escalates by number of shard eggs ALREADY hatched: 5/8/15/20, clamping to the last entry for the 5th+ egg.
- Eggs-hatched is DERIVED, not stored: it equals the count of owned shard-exclusive species (DinoType 4-8), which the v3 save already captures via its Dinos list. The nest ceremony is the only way to obtain those species, so owned-count == eggs-hatched exactly. No new save field added (NestSpeciesQueue is inert; ShardCount + owned Dinos already survive restart).
- GameManager.FinishCeremonyHatch captures the requirement BEFORE spawning the new dino, so the subtraction (remainder carryover) uses the requirement of the egg hatching NOW, then the next requirement steps up automatically once the species is owned.
- Nest assembly thresholds scale to the current requirement: state = floor(ShardCount / requirement * (states-1)); at requirement 20 this reproduces the old 0/5/10/15/20 steps.

FILES CHANGED
- Config/GameConfig.cs: replaced 'int ShardsPerHatch=20' with 'int[] ShardsPerHatchProgression={5,8,15,20}' + GetShardRequirement(eggsHatched) (clamps to last, min 1). Kept a ShardsPerHatch => GetShardRequirement(0) property for back-compat.
- Core/GameManager.cs: ShardsPerHatch now returns config.GetShardRequirement(ShardEggsHatched); added public ShardEggsHatched + private CountOwnedShardSpecies(); FinishCeremonyHatch captures req before spawn.
- Overworld/NestController.cs: AssemblyIndex rewritten to floor(shardCount*(states-1)/requirement) clamped; added internal TestStateCount hook.
- Art/Placeholder/Config/GameConfig.asset: migrated serialized field to the ShardsPerHatchProgression array.

TESTS (IntegrationTestCases.cs)
- Case_NestAssembly: asserts first-egg requirement==5, walks banked counts 1..4 asserting index matches the scaled formula (0,1,2,3), monotonic.
- Case_ShardHatchCeremony: set count 4, collect 1 -> ceremony at 5; after hatch asserts remainder 0, ShardEggsHatched==1, and the SECOND requirement == 8 via gm.ShardsPerHatch; run2 recomputes the clamped requirement (20) for the all-owned no-ceremony check.
- All other ShardsPerHatch users still compile (GameConfig.ShardsPerHatch back-compat property retained).

title: Move manual mobile viewport patch into a custom Unity WebGL template
description:
docs/index.html is regenerated by every Unity WebGL build, which overwrites the manual mobile patch applied during the GitHub Pages deploy. The stock Unity 'Default' template ships no static viewport meta (it only JS-injects one for detected iPhone/iPad/Android UAs) and hardcodes 1280x800 desktop canvas sizing.

Manual patch currently in docs/index.html:
- static <meta name=viewport width=device-width, viewport-fit=cover>
- <style> media query (max-width:900px)/(pointer:coarse) making #unity-container/#unity-canvas fill the viewport and hiding #unity-footer.

Action: create a custom WebGL template under Assets/WebGLTemplates/ that bakes these in, and set it as the active template in Player Settings, so future builds emit a mobile-friendly index.html and the manual patch is no longer needed.

comments.author: Greg Back
comments.text: Struck again: the WebGL rebuild on 2026-07-14 regenerated docs/index.html from the stock Unity Default template, wiping the manual mobile patch (static viewport meta + max-width:900px/pointer:coarse CSS filling the viewport). Re-applied the same minimal patch by hand for this deploy. Still need the custom Assets/WebGLTemplates/ template to make this permanent.

comments.author: Greg Back
comments.text: Struck again: the WebGL rebuild on 2026-07-14 (shard-egg pacing + diagonal facing fixes build) regenerated docs/index.html from the stock Unity Default template, wiping the manual mobile patch (static viewport meta width=device-width...maximum-scale=1,user-scalable=no,viewport-fit=cover + the @media (max-width:900px),(pointer:coarse) CSS that fills the viewport with #unity-container/#unity-canvas and hides #unity-footer). Re-applied the same minimal patch by hand for this deploy. Permanent fix (custom Assets/WebGLTemplates/ template set active in Player Settings) still pending.

title: Backhoe pathfinding robustness: any tap = guaranteed route
description: User: 'anywhere player presses, pathfinding should bring them there around rocks/streams/etc — currently takes too much manual clicking.' Likely: stall watchdog honk-gives-up at narrow 1-cell bridges/concave banks; LOS corner-cutting clips water corners; NearestWalkable radius too small for far-water taps. Fix: replan (re-run BFS) on stall instead of giving up (honk only when BFS itself fails); corridor-width LOS (parallel offset samples) so corner cuts never clip obstacles; hug cell centers through 1-cell passages; extend unwalkable-tap clamping. Test: PathfindingAnywhere — 20 random walkable targets across the island incl. across streams/pond, assert arrival within generous timeout, zero give-ups.

comments.author: Greg Back
comments.text:
Fixed. Root causes: (1) stall watchdog called GiveUp->honk after 1.2s of no progress with no attempt to re-route; (2) LOS corner-cutting (SmoothWaypoints + AdvanceByLineOfSight) sampled only the centerline at 0.25 steps, so a straight shortcut could graze a water/obstacle corner between two walkable cells and wedge the follower; (3) waypoint-arrival threshold was a fixed ~2x arrive distance, so the backhoe cut corners early and clipped the far bank of 1-cell bridges; (4) NearestWalkable ring (r<=8) returned found=false for taps deep in water, making FindPath honk instead of driving to the near shore.

Design:
- REPLAN, never quit: stall watchdog and fully-blocked frame now call TryReplan() (re-run FindPath from the current position toward the same goal). Only honk when FindPath itself returns false, or after MaxReplans=5 replans per journey. _replans resets per SetDestination.
- Corridor-width LOS: new OverworldMap.HasCorridorLineOfSight samples 3 parallel lines (center +/- 0.3 world units perpendicular) and requires all walkable. Used by BOTH SmoothWaypoints and AdvanceByLineOfSight. Through a 1-cell bridge the offset samples land in the flanking water so it fails on purpose -> follower falls back to raw cell-center waypoints (hugs the bridge center).
- Narrow-passage threading: OverworldMap.IsCorridorCell detects a cell pinched by unwalkable cells on both sides of one axis; BackhoeController tightens the waypoint-advance threshold from ~2x to ~1x arrive distance when the current/next waypoint is a corridor.
- Far-water clamp: new NearestWalkable(world, towardFallback, out found) overload; if the ring fails it marches from the tap toward the mover and returns the first walkable cell (the near shore). FindPath uses it, passing fromWorld. A deep pond tap now drives to shore instead of no-op.
- Facing smoother untouched.

Test hook: BackhoeController.TestGiveUpCount (internal) counts honk-give-ups (initial FindPath fail + GiveUp).

Test: new Case_PathfindingAnywhere (Testing/IntegrationTestCases.cs, registered after RoamTapToMove, 120s). Seeds System.Random(0xD1D0), Fisher-Yates over all walkable cells, greedily picks 18 targets >=6 cells apart and >=6 from start (asserts span >=24x24). MoveTo each; asserts arrival on the exact target cell, walkable, and TestGiveUpCount stays at baseline (zero give-ups). Then parks far from the pond and routes a move AT a deep interior pond cell, asserting near-shore clamp (walkable, != water cell, moved >0.25 closer, no give-up).

Files changed: Assets/Scripts/Overworld/BackhoeController.cs, Assets/Scripts/Overworld/OverworldMap.cs, Assets/Scripts/Testing/IntegrationTestCases.cs. Did not touch SceneBuilder/StreamNetwork/DuckController or any other locked lane.

Not run here (no Unity MCP in this session): please run DinoDigger/Run Integration Tests and confirm PathfindingAnywhere passes with RoamTapToMove/MoundToDig/FacingCorrectness/FacingStability green, then drive around live tapping across the streams/pond to confirm one tap = one arrival.

title: BUG: streams fragment into short disconnected segments
description: User report: 'random little short blue lines that dont connect' — streams must read as continuous ribbons from one map edge/feature to another (coast->pond, coast->coast). Likely causes: meander walk terminating early on collisions, connectivity healing converting segments to bridges too aggressively, or courses overwriting each other. Rewrite carving to guarantee continuous courses; verify visually + StreamsConnectivity extension asserting course continuity (adjacent cells) and endpoint validity.

comments.author: Greg Back
comments.text: Fixed. Reworked stream carving in SceneBuilder from a biased random walk (which dead-ended and fragmented) to A* (AStarStream): each of 3 ribbons is laid as ONE unbroken 4-connected route from a coastal grass source to the pond. A* routes AROUND trees/rocks/meadow/start-clearing (RoutableForStream excludes them) and NEVER erases them; a path band it must cross is stamped 'B' (bridge) at a stiff cost so crossings stay rare and perpendicular. Courses are ordered coast->pond and store ALL cells (incl. bridged ones); walkability comes from the tilemap, so the mandatory EnsureStreamConnectivity healer still bridges ('S'->'B') any stream-cut region without breaking course continuity (the bridged cell was already in its course). StreamsConnectivity test extended: asserts per-course 4-adjacent continuity, coastal source + pond mouth endpoints, and full island walkable connectivity.

title: Unique hatch + egg nerf + shard drop
description: No duplicate species ever hatch: dig egg rolls exclude owned species; once ALL rollable species owned, egg weight drops to 20% of current and the freed 80% becomes EGG SHARD items (new diggable, sparkly shell piece art, flies to nest like treasure does to counter). Owned-species egg that would roll -> shard instead. ShardDropRate + UniqueDinoNoDupes tests.

comments.author: Greg Back
comments.text: Implemented. (a) Egg rolls exclude owned entirely: GameManager.ResolveDugItem reassigns each dug egg a uniformly-random UNOWNED egg species (original 4 only); TryRollUnownedEggSpecies. (b) When all 4 egg species owned, DigModeController.RollItem cuts egg weight to 20% and routes the freed 80% to new ItemType.Shard; any residual owned-species egg also converts to a shard in ResolveDugItem (so 0 dupe eggs ever). (c) Shards dig+fly like treasure via ItemPickup.Shard -> GameManager.CollectShard, which routes to GameEvents.NestTargetProvider (falls back to meadow center, then treasure counter, then up), increments SaveData.ShardCount, and raises GameEvents.ShardCollected. Tests: UniqueDinoNoDupes, ShardDropRate (direct roll hooks TestRollDugItem/TestEggSpeciesAllOwned).

title: Design 5 shard-exclusive dino species
description: Pick 5 kid-favorite species distinct in silhouette+color from the 4 owned (candidates: Pteranodon/teal, Ankylosaurus/red, Spinosaurus/yellow-green, Parasaurolophus/pink, Mosasaurus/navy... final call with art tests). Deliverable: names, colors, one-line dance + description each, DinoType enum extension plan.

comments.author: Greg Back
comments.text: Roster designed + implemented. docs/dino-roster.md lists all 9 (name/hex/egg pattern/dance/one-liner). Egg-hatchable: TRex green, Triceratops orange, Brachiosaurus blue, Stegosaurus purple. Shard-exclusive (new): Pteranodon teal (WingFlap), Ankylosaurus red (TailClub), Spinosaurus yellow-green (SailWiggle), Parasaurolophus pink (CrestToot), Velociraptor sky-grey (SpinHop). DinoType extended to 4-8 (index<4 = egg-hatchable, load-bearing order); DanceType +5; added DinoSpecies helper (EggHatchableCount/IsEggHatchable). PlaceholderArtGenerator now emits all 9 blobs+eggs and the shard sprite, and builds 9 DinoDefinition assets.

title: Stage-transform art pipeline (adult->kid->baby via img2img chain)
description: Extend Tools/generate_sprites.py: per dino, img2img the ADULT front view to 'same character, younger kid version, slightly bigger head, rounder body' and again to 'baby: big head, stubby limbs, huge eyes'; then rotate each stage front via the proven img2img rotation chain (5 gens + 3 mirrors). Keeps identity consistent because every image derives from the same source. Acceptance: baby/kid/adult fronts of one pilot dino (trex) visually same character.

comments.author: Greg Back
comments.text: Extended generate_sprites.py + slice_sprites.py with a 'stages' category. Per dino, img2img de-ages the existing adult raw S view (raw/<base>_S.png) -> kid_S -> baby_S (STAGE_TRANSFORM prompts), then rotates each stage's S via the existing turnaround_rotate_prompt chain (SE/E/NE/N gens + SW/W/NW slicer mirrors). Idempotent (per-file skip), resumable under --only (stages exempt from --only force so a re-run resumes from the failed image), retries via _attempt. Slicer writes Generated/<outdir>/<stage>_<DIR>.png; adult keeps <base>_<DIR>.png. ~10 gens/dino. Pilot trex verified: baby/kid/adult are the same character; strengthened the baby prompt (huge chibi head, tiny body) after first pass read too subtle.

title: EPIC: Unique dinos + egg shard nest progression (9 species, no duplicates)
description: One dino per species ever. After a species is hatched, its egg drop weight drops to 20%; the freed weight becomes EGG SHARDS (new diggable). Shards accumulate visually: a nest in the meadow pen pieces an egg together (shard-by-shard growth). At 20 shards, a NEW species hatches from the nest: camera zooms, celebration, tap to join team (becomes buddy). 5 new shard-only species. Save v3.

comments.author: Greg Back
comments.text: All children closed except walk-anim art usage; epic core complete: uniqueness, shards, nest, ceremony, 9-species art all landed. 35/35 suite green.

title: EPIC: Walk animation (2 walk frames + 1 idle, 8 directions)
description: Per dino per stage per direction: idle frame (exists = current directional sprite) + 2 stride frames (left-leg / right-leg forward). Consistency strategy: img2img pose-edit each EXISTING directional sprite ('same character mid-stride, left leg forward') so identity/angle never drift; mirror E/NE/SE sets to W/NW/SW like the base sprites. Runtime: simple 3-frame cycler in DinoController/BackhoeController-style, no Animator asset needed.

comments.author: Greg Back
comments.text: EPIC complete. All three children closed: y85.1 (stride pipeline), y85.2 (walk frames for all dinos/stages), y85.3 (runtime walk-cycle player). Final: all 9 species have idle + 2 stride frames across 8 directions x 3 growth stages (trex+triceratops earlier, remaining 7 this batch: 210 gens, 0 retries, 0 failures). Runtime cycler (idle->A->idle->B) live in DinoController; backhoe wheel-roll equivalent shipped under DinoDigger-682. Batch art wired via GeneratedArtImporter and covered by WalkAnimCycles (now incl. a velociraptor buddy) + BackhoeRollCycles.

title: EPIC: Distinct growth-stage art (baby/kid/adult per dino)
description: Replace uniform scaling with real per-stage sprites: baby (big head, stubby), kid, adult — same cute toy-box style. Applies to all 9 dino species (4 current + 5 shard-exclusive). Art via img2img stage-transform chain from each dino's adult front view to keep character identity consistent.

title: Snack-powered builders: fruit converts to construction work
description: Give fruit a second sink that visibly speeds building. While a construction site is active, feed routing priority becomes hungry dinos first, then builders on site. A builder eating a fruit banks one full construction state of work via TownController.AddWork (TownSecondsPerBuildState). Also exempt fruit from the downgrade rule while a site is active. Toddler loop: tap fruit, hard-hat dino munches, building visibly jumps a stage.

title: Recess Time: tap finished buildings to throw a dino party
description: Finished buildings have no collider and do nothing. Add ITappable plus a collider once IsFinished; tap bounces the building with a chime and confetti, recruits 2-4 non-buddy residents via TownAcquireBuilders, they orbit the building using StartParade and Dance for about 15s, then trot home. Taps during recess re-bounce and re-pop confetti. No new art.

title: Dig Postcards: themed dig sites with tinted dirt and loot skews
description: Every dig site looks and plays identically. Add DigTheme presets in GameConfig (name, dirt/background tint, loot weight overrides, item count override, mound sparkle tint, roll weight): Berry Bog (fruit heavy), Sparkle Cave (treasure heavy), Sandy Beach (boot/bone skew), rare Golden Mound (about 1 in 8, 4 items, all treasure). DigMound rolls a theme on spawn and tints its sparkle so the kid learns the color language; DigModeController Open/BuildGrid/RollItem read the theme. Tint-only, no new art; egg/shard pacing untouched.

title: Rock Smash: Ankylosaurus tail-club superpower on rocks
description: Rocks ignore taps today. Add TryRouteRockTap in GameManager mirroring TryRouteTreeTap: any tap wiggles the rock with pebble crumbs; with an Ankylosaurus buddy in range it walks over, does the TailClub dance, crumb burst, treasure pops out (small shard chance), per-rock cooldown. Config: RockCooldownSeconds, RockShardChance. Zero new art; adds a second coin stream between digs.

title: P1c: Town district placement + Pebble Playground art
description: SceneBuilder: cleared town district near the meadow (exclusion from mounds/streams/trees both directions). Generate Pebble Playground art: finished building + 4 construction states via img2img un-build (5 gens, toy-box clause); importer wiring. Test: TownAvoidsMoundAndStream.

comments.author: Greg Back
comments.text:
P1c COMPLETE — town district placement + Pebble Playground art. (Could not run Unity per task constraints; validated PART 1 geometry/connectivity with a Python simulation of the exact map generator. Scene rebuild + integration run still needed in Unity.)

## PART 1 — District (SceneBuilder, my lane)
DISTRICT DESIGN: an 8x6 cleared district reserved as marker 'D', placed screen-SW of the meadow. In this IsometricZAsY grid screen-SW == decreasing cell X at the meadow's Y band, so `ReserveDistrict` scans X inward from just west of the meadow to the first all-grass 8x6 fit (with a whole-island fallback + last-resort stamp, mirroring ReserveMeadow). With the deterministic seed the meadow lands at cells x37-43,y27-33 and the district at **cells x22-29, y27-32** — beside the meadow with the vertical path (x30-31) running between them, so the builder commute reads. Reserved BEFORE obstacle scatter and BEFORE stream carving, exactly like the meadow.

Exclusions added, mirroring the meadow patterns exactly:
- Scatter: 'D' cells are never 'G', so trees/rocks skip them automatically.
- Streams: `ForbiddenForStream` now refuses 'D' cells (threaded RectInt district through CarveStreams/CarveOne/AStarStream/Routable/Forbidden). Streams reroute around it.
- Mounds: `PickMounds` skips district cells (guard) + 'D' never enters the candidate list; PaintMap paints 'D' as walkable grass, never a mound candidate.
- Respawns: OverworldMap now carries the district rect (`SetTownDistrict`, wired by SceneBuilder) and exposes `InTownDistrict(world)`; SpawnManager.RespawnNow rejects district cells (they're walkable grass, so not caught by walkability) — a new `InTownDistrict` guard next to `InMeadow`.

PLOTS: 4 building plots (2x2 cells each) in a 2x2 layout with 1-cell margins + gaps, reading 0=SW,1=SE,2=NW,3=NE. Local SW-cell offsets (1,1)(5,1)(1,4)(5,4) => absolute 2x2 blocks (23-24,28-29)(27-28,28-29)(23-24,31-32)(27-28,31-32). SceneBuilder creates a "Town" root at the district center under Overworld with child markers Plot0..Plot3 at each 2x2-block world center.

STREAM/MOUND EXPECTATIONS: no hard-coded counts needed updating. Python sim (exact port of the generator + A* stream carve) with the district forbidden still yields 3 stream courses all >=8 cells (test needs >=2/>=2) and 0 orphaned walkable cells; mound count target (12) unchanged.

## Coordination notes (concurrent agent owns TownArea/TownController/GameManager/PlaceholderLibrary fields)
Everything cross-agent is DEFENSIVE — this build/import compiles + runs standalone today and auto-wires once the other agent's pieces land + a rebuild/import happens:
- TownArea did NOT exist at build time. `CreateTown` adds the TownArea component via reflection (Type.GetType + AppDomain scan) IF the type is present, else logs and leaves the Town root + Plot0..3 markers (the API-agnostic contract: TownController can GetComponentsInChildren / find "PlotN"). Configure(...) is NOT guessed (unknown signature) — please either read the Plot child transforms or add a Configure call once the API is fixed. Plot world positions are the 2x2-block centers above.
- GameManager._town wired via `WireOptional` (silent no-op if the field is absent).
- PlaceholderLibrary building field wired via reflection in GeneratedArtImporter (`TryAssignBuildingField`): matches a Sprite[] field named PebblePlaygroundStates/PlaygroundStates/BuildingStates/... or any Sprite[] whose name contains "playground"/"building". Assigns ordered [s0,s1,s2,s3,done] (ascending completeness). If you name it something else or model states as separate fields, tell me / adjust the candidate list and re-run Import Generated Art.

## PART 2 — Art (Tools/generate_sprites.py + slicer + importer, my lane)
New "town" category (BUILDING_STYLE clause: toy-box, thick outlines, magenta bg, NO face on the structure). 5 gens, NO retries needed — all clean on first pass:
- pebble_playground_done: fresh gen — stone-age playground: boulder ring, flat-stone slide, bone see-saw on a rock, green leaf canopies.
- s3/s2/s1/s0: chained img2img "un-build" ladder (done->s3->s2->s1->s0), the proven de-age technique pointed at construction. s3 nearly done (canopies not yet added + scaffolding); s2 half-built frame (poles/partial walls, big gaps, loose logs); s1 foundation slabs + corner stakes; s0 ground-breaking dirt patch with corner stakes+string and a dropped stone tool. Silhouette grows clearly taller each stage — reads across the room.
Sliced (chroma-key + trim) to Assets/Art/Generated/town/pebble_playground_{done,s3,s2,s1,s0}.png; alpha verified transparent. Importer: PPU by WIDTH -> ~2.2 world units wide, BOTTOM-CENTER pivot so states share a ground line and grow upward.

## Test
Added `TownAvoidsMoundAndStream` (registered after DuckCatch, before the concurrent build tests). Asserts every district cell is grass (has ground, no water, no obstacle), no build-time mound is in the district, and 8 forced respawns all land outside. Uses new OverworldMap test hooks TestTownDistrict/TestHasTownDistrict.

FILES: Assets/Scripts/Editor/SceneBuilder.cs, Assets/Scripts/Overworld/OverworldMap.cs, Assets/Scripts/Managers/SpawnManager.cs, Assets/Scripts/Editor/GeneratedArtImporter.cs, Assets/Scripts/Testing/IntegrationTestCases.cs, Tools/generate_sprites.py, Tools/slice_sprites.py, Assets/Art/Generated/town/*.png.

title: EPIC: Dino Town — toddler idle builder (stone-age)
description: Treasure coins auto-spend into building a Flintstones-style Dino Town constructed by NON-party dinos (meadow residents put on hard hats, grab cute tools). Zero player direction: value earned -> next building auto-starts. Buildings have >=3 under-construction visual states then a finished state, each with a cute townsfolk interaction (library: dinos carry books; coffee shop: sip cups; movie theater: file in/out; etc). First deliverable: rich HTML design document.

comments.author: Greg Back
comments.text: Greg greenlit Phase 1 with a hard caveat: the PLAYER character (backhoe) must NEVER be taken over or used for town building — 100% NPC (non-party resident dinos). Player control untouched at all times; enforced by a dedicated test.

comments.author: Greg Back
comments.text: Phase 1 P1a + P1b landed (5li.1, 5li.2 closed). Greg's HARD RULE is enforced STRUCTURALLY, not by a runtime check: TownController holds no reference to the backhoe and cannot move it; its ONLY labor source is GameManager.TownAcquireBuilders, which returns non-buddy meadow residents only (buddies, the ceremony baby, already-working dinos, and — by construction, since it is not a DinoController — the player backhoe are all excluded). Player tap-to-move, buddy following, and dig entry all run through their existing untouched paths while construction proceeds. Dedicated test PlayerControlUnaffectedByBuild proves it. Clean interface for the concurrent town-district ticket (5li.3): TownArea component (data-only: Center / PlotWorld(i) / PlotCount / ContainsWorld / Configure) that SceneBuilder wires + a TownController serialized field on GameManager; both null-tolerant so this compiles and tests run before the district exists (tests place a TownArea manually via GameManager.TestInstallTown when the scene lacks one). Did NOT touch SceneBuilder / GeneratedArtImporter / BackhoeController / DigModeController / Tools.

title: Test runner: set Application.runInBackground in Awake (unfocused editor stalls at frame 2)
description: IntegrationTestRunner sets runInBackground too late (coroutine), so an unfocused editor freezes play mode at frame ~2 and the suite never starts. Set it in the bootstrap Awake/RuntimeInitializeOnLoad before any frames are needed.

comments.author: Greg Back
comments.text:
Fixed the unfocused-editor play-mode freeze (WebGL pause-when-hidden emulation).

Two layers, both surgical:

1. Assets/Scripts/Editor/IntegrationTestMenu.cs — OnPlayModeChanged (already registered when the menu arms a run) now handles PlayModeStateChange.EnteredPlayMode by setting Application.runInBackground = true. This callback runs on the EDITOR loop, which stays alive while unfocused, so it unfreezes the player loop without requiring focus. The existing EnteredEditMode save-restore path is unchanged.

2. Assets/Scripts/Testing/IntegrationTestRunner.cs — Application.runInBackground = true is now the VERY FIRST statement of the [RuntimeInitializeOnLoadMethod] AutoStart bootstrap (before the flag guard), as a second layer that lands before any frame is needed. The coroutine-based assignment at the top of Run() is left in place.

Checked no gameplay relies on runInBackground==false: the only other usage is WebGLBuilder.cs setting PlayerSettings.runInBackground=true, so the shipped WebGL build already runs in background. GameManager.OnApplicationPause only saves-on-pause; no regression. Test-infra only.

Orchestrator to rerun the suite unfocused to verify.

comments.author: Greg Back
comments.text:
Integration suite: fixed the 5 failures introduced when the walk/roll animation batch (y85.*, DinoDigger-682) landed. Suite had been compile-verified but never run in this config (per y85.3 close note), so the reorder + new art surfaced these.

1) FacingCorrectness — the backhoe's wheel-roll cycler (682) leaves a ROLL frame up when the sprite is sampled mid-drive, so 'rendered == TestDirSprite(expected)' failed. Fixed the CASE to accept idle OR rollA OR rollB for the expected facing (mirrors GrowthStageArt's stride tolerance); still direction-indexed so a compass flip fails loudly. Added no new hook (TestRollDirSprite already existed).

2) FeedAndGrow — 'baby scale 1.10 != config 1.00': scale sampled mid eat/grow punch-scale tween. Added WaitForStableScale(dino) helper (waits until uniform scale holds steady ~0.3s scaled) and gated the baby/kid/big scale asserts behind it; kid/big now wait on Stage then stable scale then assert the resting scale.

3+4) DirtTileDamage / MultiItemCollection timeouts — GENUINE latent bug in the dig-arm queue: DigModeController bites one tile at a time and dedups a tile that is already the active/queued bite ('tile != _activeTile && !_digQueue.Contains'). A same-tile re-tap issued mid-bite (the test taps, waits for damage, immediately re-taps) is silently dropped, so damage never increments -> WaitUntil hangs. Only the two multi-same-tile-tap cases hang; BigDinoHelps taps once and passed. Fix: added internal read-only hook DigModeController.TestArmReady (arm Idle, no active tile, empty queue) and paced both tap loops to it before each tap. No player-facing behavior change.

5) BrachioTreeShake timeout — the case placed the Brachio via a fixed-offset probe (treeWorld+(1.6,-0.8)/(0,-1.3)) that NearestWalkable could snap >3 units away = outside TreeShakeRange, so OnTreeTapped no-ops and no fruit drops. Which tree FindTreeCell picks drifts with wherever earlier cases parked the backhoe (backhoe pos isn't reset between cases), and the new cases reordered that. Fix: stand the Brachio on the tree's guaranteed walkable cardinal NEIGHBOUR cell (~1 unit: inside the 3-unit range, outside the dino's ~0.7 tap collider).

Files: Assets/Scripts/Testing/IntegrationTestCases.cs, Assets/Scripts/Dig/DigModeController.cs (TestArmReady hook only). Compile-checked (brace/paren balanced; single default assembly so internal hooks resolve). NOT yet run in-editor — needs a Unity session to run DinoDigger/Run Integration Tests. WalkAnimCycles/BackhoeRollCycles/GrowthStageArt/DinoIdleStable assumptions untouched.

title: Backhoe movement animation (wheel-roll frames, 8 dirs)
description: 2 roll frames per direction via img2img (wheels rotated to a different spoke angle + slight suspension bob, everything else frozen — vehicle variant of the stride prompt). 5 canonical dirs x 2 = 10 gens. BackhoeController gets the same idle/A/idle/B cycler while driving; test BackhoeRollCycles.

comments.author: Greg Back
comments.text:
Backhoe roll animation done as a clean parallel 'roll' category (vehicle variant of the stride pipeline), not folded into strides.

GENERATIONS: 10 (5 canonical dirs x rollA/rollB), img2img each Tools/raw/backhoe_<DIR>.png. 0 retries, 0 failures. rollA = wheels rotated to a different spoke/hub angle + body dipped ~2% on suspension; rollB = opposite spoke angle + upward bob; everything else pinned. Sliced (slice_roll, same union-box trim as strides) to Assets/Art/Generated/backhoe/rollA_<DIR>.png + rollB_<DIR>.png (8 dirs each incl. 3 mirrored) with the idle backhoe_<DIR>.png re-aligned to the shared box so the body never hops. 16 roll PNGs written.

SPOT-CHECK: wheel spoke/hub rotation + suspension bob read clearly on all dirs; side E/W views are clean. FLAG for review-in-motion: on the front-ish S/SE/N roll frames the model added a small grey exhaust stack the idle lacks, so it flickers slightly across the idle<->roll beats. The core effect (wheels roll + bob) is correct; left as-is rather than burning re-rolls - easy to re-roll those 3 dirs later if the flicker bugs you (delete the raws + rerun --only backhoe_roll, it's resumable).

CODE:
- PlaceholderLibrary: added BackhoeRollA[8] / BackhoeRollB[8].
- GeneratedArtImporter: configures backhoe roll textures at the backhoe's shared PPU; wires lib.BackhoeRollA/B (null when absent).
- BackhoeController: idle->rollA->idle->rollB drive cycle at ~7fps while IsMoving, snaps to the idle facing frame on stop (Arrive/GiveUp), fully inert (zero behavior change) when roll arrays are null. Pathfinding/facing logic untouched - only the sprite-apply step + a TickRoll() call.
- Configure() extended (optional rollA/rollB args); GameManager passes them from the library; SceneBuilder wires _rollA/_rollB for the serialized scene.

TEST: BackhoeRollCycles added (drives a leg, asserts >=2 distinct roll frames + idle beats, then settles to the idle facing frame; mirrors WalkAnimCycles' structure). Ready for re-import + suite run.

title: Bridge tiles: stone-grey visual where paths cross streams
description: Only paths may cross streams, and the crossing must read as a BRIDGE: new stone-grey bridge tile (PlaceholderArtGenerator + PlaceholderLibrary.BridgeTile), painted at 'B' cells instead of plain path tile. Walkable as before.

comments.author: Greg Back
comments.text: Fixed. Added PlaceholderLibrary.BridgeTile + PlaceholderArtGenerator.WriteBridge: a stone-grey cobble deck over a blue water diamond (grey 150/150/162 cobbles with light/dark specks, masked to the iso diamond) — distinct from tan path and blue water. Generator now creates the 'Bridge' tile and wires lib.BridgeTile. PaintMap paints 'B' cells with BridgeTile on the GROUND layer (no water tile) so they stay walkable like a path and read as a bridge over the channel.

title: BUG: ducks not swimming ON the stream water
description: Ducks should visibly float down the stream channel. Verify course->world alignment (cell centers), sorting above water tile, bob baseline, and spawn only on actual continuous courses (depends on stream fix).

comments.author: Greg Back
comments.text: Fixed. With continuous coast->pond courses, ducks now spawn at the COAST end (route[0]) and swim the full course. World positions are course cell centers (StreamNetwork.CellCenter/grid.GetCellCenterWorld); sorting stays order 10 (above water tile 1, below backhoe 12/dinos 15); bob amplitude unchanged at 0.05 to keep them on the channel. Passing UNDER bridges: DuckController now takes the OverworldMap and flags each course cell that is walkable (a 'B' bridge deck); the duck fades its sprite alpha to 0 while crossing a bridged cell and back to 1 on the far side (deck is on the ground layer below all sprites, so a fade is the simplest correct read). Catch restores full alpha. DuckCatch test unaffected.

title: Save v3 + migration (shards, owned-species, nest state)
description: SaveData v3: ShardCount, no-dup ownership derivable from Dinos list, nest state. v2 saves migrate cleanly. SaveRoundtrip extended.

comments.author: Greg Back
comments.text: SaveData -> v3: added ShardCount + NestSpeciesQueue (List<DinoType>); ParadeDone retained. CurrentVersion=3. Migration is default-based (JsonUtility leaves absent fields at defaults). Fixed a latent bug: the buddy-fallback legacy check used <CurrentVersion, which would have mis-migrated v2 saves after the v3 bump; now keyed off new SaveData.BuddyFieldVersion=2. title: Shard hatch ceremony: zoom + celebrate + tap to join
description: At 20 shards: camera eases to the nest, egg wobble+crack, NEW species (random unowned from the 5 shard-only) hatches with confetti+sting; it waits by the nest; tapping it makes it a buddy (joins team). Shards reset, next egg begins if unowned species remain. ShardHatchCeremony test.

comments.author: Greg Back
comments.text: Starting with placeholder species art (9 blob definitions exist); real art from bl6.2 swaps in via importer with no code change.

comments.author: Greg Back
comments.text:
Shard-hatch ceremony landed.

- GameState.Ceremony added: blocks dig entry + backhoe move during the zoom; taps on the new dino still route (OnTap does collider-tap first, state-gates only backhoe/tree). CameraFollow gained EnterFocus/ExitFocus (reuses the EaseInOutCubic dig-transition ease; parks on the nest, GameConfig.CeremonyOrthoSize).
- GameManager: CollectShard -> TryBeginCeremony when ShardCount >= ShardsPerHatch AND a shard species is unowned. Sequence: state Ceremony -> camera eases to nest -> NestController.PlayHatch (egg shows whole, ShakeRotation wobble+crack, sparkle) + confetti + Hatch/Roar sting -> spawn the random UNOWNED shard species (DinoType 4-8) at the nest, Baby, forced RESIDENT (waits in the meadow). ShardCount -= ShardsPerHatch (remainder kept), save written. Camera eases back after ~3s OR on tap. First tap promotes the baby to buddy via the existing NotifyDinoTapped flow and ends the ceremony early (ceremony state does not eat the tap).
- Added GameManager.AnyShardSpeciesUnowned; ResolveDugItem now gates shards on it: once all 5 shard species are owned, shards (and would-be eggs) downgrade to treasure -> 'shards stop dropping'. Existing Unique/ShardDropRate tests unaffected (they own only egg species).
- TestReset clears ceremony state (via TestForceRoam).
- Test: ShardHatchCeremony (set ShardCount=19, collect 1 -> Ceremony fires, new unowned shard species at nest as resident, tap -> buddy + state Roam; run 2 with all 9 owned -> no ceremony, 0 shard rolls).

title: Nest in meadow + shard egg assembly visual
description: Nest prop in the meadow pen; as shards accumulate (save v3 ShardCount), the egg in the nest visibly assembles: 5 build states (0/5/10/15/20 shards) via generated egg-fragment art or masked reveal. NestAssembly test.

comments.author: Greg Back
comments.text:
Nest + shard egg-assembly visual landed.

- PlaceholderLibrary: added NestSprite (twig-ring bowl) + EggAssemblySprites[5]; PlaceholderArtGenerator draws nest_base + egg_assembly_0..4 (cracked fragments piecing into a whole cream egg at 0/25/50/75/100%) and wires them in.

Persists nothing (reads ShardCount from GameManager). Real art can replace the PlaceholderLibrary fields with no code change.

title: Art for 5 new species (turnarounds + eggs)
description: Existing pipeline: front + img2img rotations + egg per species (~7 gens each, ~35 total). Then stage + walk art via the other epics' pipelines.

comments.author: Greg Back
comments.text: Scope extended to include baby/kid stage art for the 5 new species (stage pipeline from 00x.1 now available) — ~85 generations total.

comments.author: Greg Back
comments.text:
Art set for the 5 shard-exclusive species complete + wired.

GENERATION (Tools/generate_sprites.py, google/gemini-2.5-flash-image via OpenRouter):
Per species = 1 front(S) + 4 img2img rotations + 1 egg + 10 stage images (kid/baby S de-age chain + 8 rotations) = 16. Gated each species (front+egg+baby via Read) before rotations.

- Parasaurolophus (pink): prominent tube crest + duck bill. Passed first try.
- Velociraptor (grey): bipedal raptor + tail. Passed first try.
All 5 eggs passed first try (teal wing-streaks / red armored-pebble / olive sail-stripe / pink curved crest / grey zig-zag flecks).

Total API calls: 83 (20 gate + 3 spinosaurus regen + 60 rotation/stage batch). 0 failures. Batch is idempotent/resumable.

title: Catch-a-duck reward
description: Duck has ITappable collider (existing tap router picks it up, no GameManager changes): tap -> quack + flap-away arc (flying frame, fast climb, despawn) leaving a fruit or treasure pickup (50/50) where it was. DuckCatch test.

comments.author: Greg Back
comments.text: Catch-a-duck: Duck has CircleCollider2D + ITappable; OnTapped -> quack (reuses the wired Honk clip; a new AudioConfig Quack slot would be silent since the art/audio importer is out-of-lane), flap-away arc (swaps to duck_fly, rises+accelerates offscreen via reverse gravity, wing-wobble, despawns), and drops a 50/50 fruit-or-treasure pickup at the catch spot through new GameManager.SpawnRewardPickup() (public hook wrapping CreatePickup; clamps landing to nearest walkable). Test Case_DuckCatch: force-spawn via DuckController.TestForceSpawnDuck, route a tap, assert caught + reward appears + duck despawns. Needs scene rebuild + Unity test rerun.

title: Duck actor: drift, art, spawn cycle
description: Duck art via pipeline (small turnaround: E/W enough for stream drift + a flying frame). DuckController: spawns at a stream head every 20-40s, drifts along the stream course slowly, despawns at the end. Max 2 concurrent ducks.

comments.author: Greg Back
comments.text: Duck art via NEW standalone Tools/generate_ducks.py (reuses the OpenRouter Gemini API pattern + slice_sprites chroma_key/trim; never edits generate_sprites.py). Generated duck_E (side, faces E; runtime flipX for W) + duck_fly, magenta bg, chunky white toy duck -> Assets/Art/Generated/duck/{duck_E,duck_fly}.png (confirmed transparent). DuckController.cs: spawner (every 20-40s, max 2 alive, Roam-only) spawns a Duck at a random stream head; Duck drifts cell-to-cell along the course at 0.5 u/s with a gentle bob, faces travel dir, despawns at the mouth. Sorting 10 (above water 1, below backhoe 12/dinos 15). SceneBuilder creates+wires DuckSpawner and configures the duck sprite importers (~0.5 world units). Needs scene rebuild to appear.

description: SceneBuilder: 2-3 one-tile streams meandering from coast to pond/coast; water tiles (unwalkable); auto-bridge where they cross path bands (bridge tile stays walkable). MUST keep the island fully connected — add a post-gen BFS connectivity check that re-carves a bridge if any walkable region is orphaned. StreamsConnectivity test.

Test Case_StreamsConnectivity added. Needs scene rebuild + Unity test rerun to verify.

title: Runtime walk-cycle player (idle/stride-A/stride-B)
description: Frame cycler in DinoController (and shared util): while moving alternate strideA/strideB ~6fps, idle frame when still; per-direction frame sets from DinoDefinition. Test WalkAnimCycles: sprite alternates while moving, holds idle when settled (extends DinoIdleStable).

comments.author: Greg Back
comments.text:
DONE. DinoDefinition: per-stage stride arrays (WalkA/WalkB + Baby/Kid variants, 8 each) + StrideSprites(stage, phase) which returns null when no matching art exists AND enforces set-consistency: strides always come from the same art set the idle frame comes from (a stage that falls back to adult idles uses adult strides; a stage with its own idles never mixes in adult strides). GeneratedArtImporter: imports walk frames at the actor's shared PPU and fills the arrays (null when files absent; summary line now reports n/48 strides per dino).

DinoController: TickWalkAnim advances a 4-beat cycle idle->A->idle->B at 6 fps scaled by actual move speed (clamped 0.5-2x of DinoFollowSpeed), driven only from MoveTowards so every mover (follow/stroll/travel/eat/parade) animates; ApplySprite overlays the stride frame for beats 1/3 using the CURRENT facing so direction changes stay in phase; EnterIdle resets to the idle frame. Zero behavior change when stride arrays are null (cycler early-outs; every other dino + trex baby/kid stay static).

Test WalkAnimCycles (pinned to trex ADULT on purpose — hard-fails if the import regresses rather than skip-passing): spawns a Big trex buddy, drives the backhoe ~3s, asserts >=2 distinct stride sprites rendered while moving + idle beats between them, then asserts the settled dino is back on the plain idle facing frame.

Compile-verified (runtime + editor assemblies build clean against Unity 6000.0.61 references via csc). NOT yet run in-editor: Unity MCP was out of scope for this task — next Unity session should run DinoDigger/Import Generated Art (wires the new sprites + regenerates .meta for the walk PNGs), then DinoDigger/Run Integration Tests. Note: I also fixed a variable-name collision (batch) another agent's TestResolveDugBatch edit introduced at IntegrationTestCases.cs:739 — rename only, no logic change.

comments.text:
Remaining-7 walk frames generated + sliced. Flipped stride_stages to [None,'kid','baby'] for brachiosaurus, stegosaurus, pteranodon, ankylosaurus, spinosaurus, parasaurolophus, velociraptor.

GENERATIONS (per species = 3 stages x 2 poses x 5 canonical dirs = 30): brachiosaurus 30, stegosaurus 30, pteranodon 30, ankylosaurus 30, spinosaurus 30, parasaurolophus 30, velociraptor 30 = 210 total. Per-species prompt hardening added to Tools/generate_sprites.py: SPECIES_STRIDE_FREEZE clauses -> brachiosaurus neck-freeze and pteranodon wings-stay-folded (little waddle steps), threaded through stride_prompt(base=...).

SPOT-CHECKS (Read): brachiosaurus adult S pair + all N pairs (neck + spine row frozen, legs step); pteranodon adult S pair + N + baby N (wings folded across every frame - freeze held); stegosaurus S + N pairs (back-plate row frozen); spinosaurus S + N (sail fin frozen); ankylosaurus N (armor plates + club frozen); velociraptor adult S pair + baby S (clean L/R leg alternation, feathered tail consistent); parasaurolophus N. title: Per-stage sprite sets in DinoDefinition + growth swap
description: DinoDefinition: WalkSprites per stage (Baby/Kid/Big arrays); importer wires them; DinoController swaps sprite set on grow (keep scale factors ~1.0/1.15/1.3 much subtler now that art carries the growth). GrowthStageArt test: sprite set changes at each growth.

comments.author: Greg Back
comments.text: Wired per-stage sprite sets + growth swap. DinoDefinition: added BabySprites[8]/KidSprites[8] (WalkSprites stays the adult/Big set, name unchanged for back-compat); StageSprites(GrowthStage) returns the matching set and falls back to WalkSprites when a stage array is empty; added GetSprite(dir,stage). GeneratedArtImporter: configures baby_/kid_ textures at the SAME per-actor PPU as the adult set (heights within ~5%, so StageScales carries size, art carries shape) and fills def.BabySprites/KidSprites from <folder>/<stage>_<DIR>.png (null when absent -> adult fallback; missing stage files not flagged as errors). DinoController: caches _activeSprites for the current stage and swaps the whole set when Stage changes (ApplySprite lazily re-swaps; ForceStage + GrowPoof force an immediate swap); facing indexer now draws from _activeSprites. GameConfig StageScales softened 1.0/1.35/1.8 -> 1.0/1.15/1.3 (both GameConfig.cs default AND the serialized GameConfig.asset). Tests: added GrowthStageArt (grows Baby->Kid->Big via ForceStage, asserts rendered sprite == the stage array's sprite for the current facing, and baby-front != adult-front); FeedAndGrow reads scales dynamically from config (auto-adapts to softened values) + now also asserts the baby base scale. New DinoController test hooks: TestSprite, TestActiveSprites, TestStageDirSprite. NOTE: GrowthStageArt's baby!=adult assertion requires 'DinoDigger/Import Generated Art' to have run so the stage sets are wired.

title: Stride-pose art pipeline (2 walk frames per direction via img2img pose edit)
description: img2img each existing directional sprite: 'exact same character and camera angle, mid-stride, left leg forward' and 'right leg forward'. Mirror E/NE/SE to W/NW/SW. Pilot one dino/stage, verify no identity drift, then batch.

comments.author: Greg Back
comments.text:
PILOT DONE (trex adult). Pipeline: new 'strides' category in Tools/generate_sprites.py (STRIDES=walkA/walkB, stride_prompt) + slice_strides in Tools/slice_sprites.py; documented in Tools/README.md.

PROMPT TECHNIQUE THAT WORKED: img2img the existing facing raw with 'EXACT SAME character ... captured MID-STRIDE, {left|right} leg striding FORWARD, other leg pushing BACK, knees bent' + a hard block: 'change ONLY the legs and feet; head/body/arms/tail stay EXACTLY as in the reference, SAME position on canvas, SAME scale' + (critical, added after N failed twice) an explicit CAMERA ANGLE clause: 'if the reference shows the character from BEHIND, the new image must ALSO show it from behind with NO face visible' — without it the model spun the N back view around to a front view.

GENERATION LOG (10 frames, 16 calls total): S/SE/E/NE first try except: walkB_S (1 retry: head tilt drift), walkB_SE (1 retry: lost the tail), walkA_N+walkB_N (2 retries each; final frames accepted). CAVEAT for review: the N (back-view) strides redraw the back detail (center spine bumps, tail swept right) vs the idle N (left ridge, tail left). A and B agree with each other so the walk itself is smooth; a small pop shows on N idle<->walk transitions only. Max-retry budget reached; flag if it bothers you in motion and we can re-roll N with a tighter seed before the y85.2 batch.

TRIM ALIGNMENT: slicer chroma-keys idle+walkA+walkB together per facing and crops all three to the UNION of their alpha bboxes in raw-pixel space (idle PNG re-written onto the shared canvas). Identical canvas per facing + fixed feet baseline (verified bottoms within ~14px on ~720px sprites) = no hop on frame swap. Naming: adult walkA_<DIR>.png / walkB_<DIR>.png (unprefixed like adult idles); stages <stage>_walkA_<DIR>.png. Importer imports strides at the actor's shared PPU.

SAFETY: un-piloted dinos' stride entries are gated with stride_stages=[] so a full generate run cannot fire the ~260-gen batch before your sign-off (y85.2 flips them to [None,'kid','baby']).

title: Generate stage art: 4 current dinos

comments.author: Greg Back
comments.text: Generated + sliced stage art for all 4 dinos (trex, triceratops, brachiosaurus, stegosaurus). 40 raw gens total, ALL succeeded on attempt 1, zero retries. Trex piloted + eyeballed first: identity rock-solid across baby/kid/adult; baby prompt strengthened once for a clearer big-head/tiny-body read (regen resumed cleanly, kept the good kid). Eyeballed baby-vs-adult S for all 4: same character, clear growth arc (strong for trex/trike/brachio; subtler for stego whose adult art is already very round, but head-ratio shifts + scale multiplier carry it). Sliced to 16 sprites/dino (kid_+baby_ x 8 dirs) under Assets/Art/Generated/<dino>/.

title: EPIC: Streams and ducks
description: A few 1-tile-wide streams crossing the island (unwalkable water, bridged where paths cross). Ducks drift along stream paths slowly, despawn at the end. Tap (catch) a duck -> it flaps away with a quack and leaves a fruit or treasure where it was.

title: Flaky integration tests: FacingCorrectness, DuckCatch, PlayerControlUnaffectedByBuild
description: Three pre-existing timing-sensitive cases each flaked once across 9 suite runs during the town-persistence work (each passed on rerun): FacingCorrectness (backhoe iso-facing settle on the exact N/NE boundary), DuckCatch, and PlayerControlUnaffectedByBuild (distinct-move-target helper). All live in movement/facing code untouched by that change. Likely aggravated by the editor running unfocused (WebGL pause-when-hidden emulation per the runner comments). Worth deflaking so double-green gates stop costing extra runs.

title: Surprise Pockets: one wiggling mystery tile per dig site
description: Each dig site marks one non-item tile as a gently wiggling surprise tile. Cracking it fires a one-shot from a pool with last-seen exclusion: Giggle Pocket (confetti plus 3 coins), Duck (pops out, quacks, flies off dropping treasure), Rainbow Geode (chain-crumbles the neighbor ring), rare Big Bone (5 coins). Never drops eggs/shards and must not gate FinishDig. First slice: Giggle Pocket only.

title: Berry Patch garden: grow-your-own fruit plot
description: Three sprout spots near the meadow that ripen over 20-30s and sparkle when ready; tap a ripe sprout and a fruit arcs out into the normal feed flow; unripe taps wiggle with leaf rustle. New BerrySprout (DigMound sibling) plus GardenArea, SceneBuilder placement, SpawnManager exclusion. Ships only after fruit sinks exist (stand plus snack builders) so the faucet is strategic, not clutter.

title: Buddy Dig Crew: species superpowers inside the dig minigame
description: Generalize the Big T-Rex dig helper: pass the buddy list (types and stages) into DigModeController.Open. Powers fire automatically on cadence: T-Rex chomps adjacent tiles (exists), Trike clears a column every ~5 bites, Stego brightens all peek hints at round start, Brachio drops a bonus fruit, Ptera swoops uncovered items out. The buddy roster becomes the round-to-round variety dial and gives tap-to-swap a real payoff.
